evidence.beauty: Fluent C1 Ukrainian seminar lesson links birth, christening, wedding, funeral, lament, and kinship voice through recurring transition images.
evidence.decolonization: The module rejects decorative ethnography and hidden older-core claims, keeping Ukrainian family rites source-bound, Christian-community-aware, and agency-centered.
evidence.engagement: Short verified fragments, careful-reading practice, inline checks, and focused workbook tasks give learners repeated evidence-based entry points.
evidence.naturalness: Learner-facing AI/build/register leakage cleanup keeps the prose in Ukrainian seminar register without process instructions.
evidence.pedagogical: Repeatable method asks learners to track action, performer, object, addressee, voice, formula, and proof limits across family rites.
evidence.review: Claude route was attempted first; AGY/Gemini fallback review blockers were fixed before final validation.
evidence.source_hygiene: Resources use public Wikisource/Wikipedia references, and the lesson marks short-fragment-only versus public-excerpt-candidate posture.
evidence.tone: Serious seminar voice holds tenderness, hierarchy, grief, memory, and community pressure without nostalgia or spectacle.
